mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling while CPIAUCSL is also decelerating, real discount-rate pressure eases and equity multiples expand. That combination predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not deliver higher 20d forward returns than unconditional baseline, or if the sign flips positive during episodes where FEDFUNDS rises and CPIAUCSL accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE indicates deteriorating household income and weaker earnings expectations, which should pressure equities lower over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are positive in most periods after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE increase, or if the signal loses directional accuracy versus baseline in late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad demand strength in retail spending and housing activity, which supports revenue expectations and lifts equities over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint high-growth RSAFS and HOUST observations fail to precede positive 60d SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect with no incremental interaction value.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO after a prior slump indicates that industrial output is reaccelerating from cyclical weakness, which should support cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% m/m after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if post-slump INDPRO rebounds do not produce higher 5d SPX returns than non-triggered periods, or if the effect is absent after repeated industrial recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% in a non-recessionary environment tightens discounting and financial conditions, reducing equity valuations and pushing forward returns negative over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:post-crisis policy rates remained near zero into early 2013
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS episodes do not show below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if returns are positive in most observations after the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in GDPC1 together with rising PAYEMS identifies a broad real-economy expansion in output and payrolls, which supports earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if combined growth in GDPC1 and PAYEMS does not outperform single-series effects or fails to raise 60d SPX return probabilities above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS turns down while UNRATE turns up, labor-market deterioration is no longer ambiguous and earnings expectations should weaken, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises in the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up regime does not produce negative 20d returns more often than not, or if the sign is not stronger than either labor proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in HOUST after a prior high level signals a housing rollover that usually reflects weaker future consumer demand and credit creation, which should pressure equities lower over the next week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% m/m from a 6-month high
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST drawdowns do not precede below-baseline 5d SPX returns, or if the signal is reversed across multiple housing slowdown episodes.
